docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Manthan

Decision Infrastructure

Every system runs on decisions. Manthan makes them deterministic, auditable, and enforceable.

Install PR Gate Live System

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
Modern systems — especially AI — operate in probabilistic generation. The same input can produce different outputs. There is no traceability. No enforcement.

This makes them unfit for critical systems.

docs.title: The Shift
docs.text:
Generation

Possibilities

Suggestions

Probabilistic

Decision

Outcomes

Enforcement

Deterministic

Key Principle

docs.title: Example Walkthrough
docs.text: This section demonstrates how Manthan produces deterministic decisions using real scenarios.

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "amount": 15000,
  "country": "high_risk",
  "user_verified": false
}

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
IF amount > 10000 → reject
IF country == high_risk → reject

docs.title: Decision Flow
docs.text:
• amount > 10000 → TRUE
  • First match → reject

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "reject",
  "reason": "amount threshold exceeded"
}

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "amount": 2000,
  "country": "low_risk",
  "user_verified": true
}

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
IF amount > 10000 → reject
ELSE → approve

docs.title: Decision Flow
docs.text:
• amount > 10000 → FALSE
  • No reject rule matched → approve

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "approve",
  "reason": "within safe limits"
}

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "user_age": 17,
  "document_verified": false
}

docs.title: Graph
docs.text:
flowchart LR

A[Age Check] --> B[Eligibility]
A --> C[Document Verification]

B --> D[Decision]
C --> D

classDef node fill:#020617,stroke:#a78bfa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
class A,B,C,D node;

docs.title: Logic
docs.text:
• user_age < 18 → not eligible
  • document_verified = false → fail

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "reject",
  "reason": "age and verification failure"
}

docs.title: Contract (v1.0)
docs.text:
rules:
  - condition: "score < 50"
    action: "reject"
  - condition: "score >= 50"
    action: "approve"

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "score": 72
}

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text:
• score < 50 → FALSE
  • score >= 50 → TRUE

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "approve",
  "contract_version": "v1.0"
}

docs.title: Determinism Proof
docs.text:
For ALL examples:

Same Input → Same Rules → Same Output

No randomness No ambiguity No hidden state

docs.title: Key Takeaway
docs.text:
Manthan converts:

Inputs → Rules → Decisions

Into a fully deterministic system.

flowchart LR

    classDef main fill:#020617,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:2px,color:#ffffff;

docs.text:
{
  "decision": "reject",
  "score": 0.92,
  "confidence": "high"
}

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Enriches decision output with metadata
  • Does not modify or override the decision
  • Operates deterministically

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• MUST NOT change decision
  • Stateless
  • Deterministic

docs.title: Boundaries
docs.text:
• No decision authority
  • No side effects
  • No persistence

docs.title: Core Statement
docs.text: Explains decisions, never makes them.

docs.title: ResearchDecision Infrastructure
docs.text:
Manthan defines a new class of systems where decisions are deterministic, auditable, and enforceable.

This research establishes decisions as a first-class primitive in software systems.

docs.title: The Premise
docs.text:
Modern AI systems generate outputs.

They do not make decisions.

  • Outputs are probabilistic
  • Behavior is non-deterministic
  • Outcomes are not enforceable

This creates a fundamental gap.

Systems can generate — but they cannot be trusted to decide.

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
Support — Manthan Support Deterministic Systems Require Deterministic Reports

Manthan enforces decisions as system truth. If something behaves unexpectedly, it must be reproducible.

🐞 Report Bug ⚙️ Contract Issue 💡 Feature Request Include: • Pull request link • Contract reference • Decision output • Trace Same input should produce the same result. If it does not, this is a system fault. Manthan — Decision Infrastructure

docs.title: System
docs.text:
System

Manthan defines how decisions are made, validated, and enforced in software systems.

The Problem

AI systems operate in probabilistic generation.

  • Same input → different outputs
  • No traceability
  • No enforcement

This makes them unreliable for decisions.

Generation vs Decision

AI operates in generation mode. Manthan operates in decision mode.

  • Generation → possibilities
  • Decision → outcomes

Prompts generate. Contracts decide.

Decision Boundary

AI is useful for ambiguity. Decisions require certainty.

AI (Generation)

  • Interpretation
  • Exploration
  • Ambiguity

Decision Systems

  • Determinism
  • Enforcement
  • Guarantees

Crossing this boundary turns insight into decision.

System Model

A Manthan system follows a deterministic structure:

  • Intent → defined as contracts
  • Contracts → define decisions
  • Decisions → executed deterministically
  • Execution → enforced by the system

Contracts

Contracts define decisions explicitly.

  • Versioned
  • Immutable
  • Deterministic

They are the source of truth.

Decisions are not inferred. They are specified.

Intelligence Layer

Decisions are made first.

  • Explanation
  • Context
  • Scoring

This layer does not influence decisions. It explains decisions — it never makes them.

Outcome

Without deterministic decisions:

  • Inconsistent outcomes
  • No traceability
  • No enforcement

With Manthan:

  • Consistent decisions
  • Full traceability
  • Enforced outcomes

Reliable systems require deterministic decisions.
